description: Create a content slide on shifting cyber risk priorities with a two-column layout: explanatory text on the left and a horizontal bar chart on the right ranking key cyber risk dimensions by percentage.

header.headline: Cyber – risk priorities are shifting toward data, social engineering and third-party exposure
header.sub: Figure 2: Which aspects of cyber risk are most important to your organization?

left.body:
CROs continue to describe cyber risk as multidimensional, with emphasis on different aspects of exposure and control depending on the region. This year’s results indicate that data privacy and protection (including regulatory compliance) is the most frequently top-ranked cyber dimension, followed by protection against phishing and third-party/vendor cyber risk management.

Regional differences also stand out. In the Americas and APAC, cyber risk is more frequently framed through technology resilience, control effectiveness and data protection, while in EMEIA, it is more closely linked to regulatory oversight and third-party obligations.

right.title: Which aspects of cyber risk are most important to your organization?

series.label: Protection against phishing, social engineering and business email compromise
Data privacy and protection (including regulatory compliance, such as GDPR, CCPA)
Protection from malware, ransomware and advanced persistent threats
Password, identity and access management (including privileged access controls)
